name: kitty
description: Linux kitty terminal tools for Codex, using numbered managed and adopted kitty remote-control sockets for terminal listing, unified input, and screen reads.

interface.shortDescription: Send input to and inspect numbered kitty terminals
interface.longDescription: Kitty gives Codex Linux-native terminal tools for using visible numbered kitty terminals such as K1. The model-facing interface exposes kitty_list for available terminals and windows, kitty_open for opening a terminal without sending input, kitty_send for shell commands, raw text, and key input with screen feedback, and kitty_read for the current terminal screen. Missing K-number targets are created as managed kitty instances, while future user-opened kitty launches can still be adopted through the user-level wrapper. Closed, unreachable, and windowless socket entries are pruned so user-closed terminals disappear from the model-facing list and do not block later reuse of the same K-number. It uses kitty's remote-control socket interface and does not change the user's global kitty configuration, use TCP sockets, retroactively adopt old terminals without sockets, steal focus when opening managed terminals, or inject hidden status commands into the visible terminal. On KWin, managed opens also best-effort restore focus to the previously active window if the terminal is activated anyway.
